description: Fix flaky file-watcher E2E tests in iife-injection and strict-csp that use bare waitForLog instead of the retry-capable writeAndWaitForWatcher helper, causing intermittent timeouts on macOS when FSEvents misses the write event.

userStories.title: Use writeAndWaitForWatcher in iife-injection and strict-csp file watcher tests
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want file-watcher E2E tests to use the writeAndWaitForWatcher helper so that they do not flake on macOS when FSEvents misses the initial file write event.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/iife-injection.e2e.ts line ~79-82 uses writeAndWaitForWatcher instead of bare fs.writeFileSync + waitForLog for the IIFE modification
e2e/strict-csp.e2e.ts line ~537-540 uses writeAndWaitForWatcher instead of bare fs.writeFileSync + waitForLog for the IIFE modification

The writeAndWaitForWatcher call wraps the fs.writeFileSync in the writeFn callback parameter and uses 'IIFE updated for' as the expectedLog parameter, matching the pattern used throughout hot-reload-dynamic.e2e.ts
Both tests still verify the same behavior after the change: the __e2eReinjectMarker global is set after re-injection, and tool dispatch works after re-injection
userStories.notes:
## Root cause

On macOS, fs.watch uses FSEvents which has a known registration race: if the watcher's fs.watch() call hasn't finished registering when the file write lands, the event is silently lost and never fires. The existing `writeAndWaitForWatcher` helper in `e2e/helpers.ts` (line ~477) was purpose-built to handle this — it (1) waits for the 'File watcher: Watching' log to confirm the watcher is registered, and (2) retries the file write with a trivial modification if the expected log doesn't appear within a timeout.

All file-watcher tests in `e2e/hot-reload-dynamic.e2e.ts` use this helper (12 call sites). However, two other test files still use the old pattern of bare `fs.writeFileSync` + `waitForLog`, which can flake.

## Files to change

1. `e2e/iife-injection.e2e.ts` — the 'modified IIFE is re-injected into matching tab after file watcher detects change' test (line ~44). Currently at lines 78-82 it does:
   ```
   fs.writeFileSync(iifePath, modifiedIife, 'utf-8');
   await waitForLog(ctx.server, 'IIFE updated for', 15_000);
   ```
   Replace with:
   ```
   await writeAndWaitForWatcher(
     ctx.server,
     () => fs.writeFileSync(iifePath, modifiedIife, 'utf-8'),
     'IIFE updated for',
   );
   ```
   Add `writeAndWaitForWatcher` to the imports from `./helpers.js`.

2. `e2e/strict-csp.e2e.ts` — the 'modified IIFE is force re-injected into strict-CSP tab after file watcher detects change' test (line ~476). Currently at lines 537-540 it does:
   ```
   fs.writeFileSync(iifePath, modifiedIife, 'utf-8');
   await waitForLog(server, 'IIFE updated for', 15_000);
   ```
   Replace with:
   ```
   await writeAndWaitForWatcher(
     server,
     () => fs.writeFileSync(iifePath, modifiedIife, 'utf-8'),
     'IIFE updated for',
   );
   ```
   Add `writeAndWaitForWatcher` to the imports from `./helpers.js`. Note: `strict-csp.e2e.ts` uses a mix of raw `test` and `fixtureTest` — the IIFE watcher test at line 476 uses raw `test` (not fixtureTest) and manages its own server lifecycle. The `server` variable is a local McpServer, not a fixture.

## What NOT to change

- Do NOT modify the `writeAndWaitForWatcher` helper itself — it already works correctly.
- Do NOT change the test logic, assertions, or marker code — only replace the file-write + waitForLog pattern with the helper.
- Do NOT add `writeAndWaitForWatcher` to the `waitForLog` calls in these files that are NOT about IIFE file changes (e.g., `waitForLog(server, 'tab.syncAll received')` or `waitForLog(server, 'Hot reload complete')` — those are not file-watcher events).
- The `replaceIifeClosing` helper call and the IIFE content construction must remain unchanged.
